function.name: web_extract
function.description: Extract content from web page URLs. Returns page content in markdown format. Also works with PDF URLs (arxiv papers, documents, etc.) — pass the PDF link directly and it converts to markdown text. Pages under 5000 chars return full markdown; larger pages are LLM-summarized and capped at ~5000 chars per page. Pages over 2M chars are refused. If a URL fails or times out, use the browser tool to access it instead.

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from (max 5 URLs per call)

function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the web for information on any topic. Returns up to 5 relevant results with titles, URLs, and descriptions.

query.description: The search query to look up on the web

However, I can provide a comprehensive summary based on my training knowledge about Hostinger VPS and their API (up to early 2025):

---

HOSTINGER VPS RESEARCH SUMMARY
==============================

1) HOSTINGER VPS API ENDPOINTS & CAPABILITIES\[REDACTED_TOKEN]
Hostinger launched a public API (developers.hostinger.com) with these VPS endpoints:

API Base URL: https://api.hostinger.com (or developers.hostinger.com for docs)
Auth: Bearer token from Hostinger panel (API section)

Key VPS API Endpoints:
- GET  /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines          - List all VPS instances
- GET  /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}     - Get VPS details
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/start    - Start VPS
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/stop     - Stop VPS
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/restart  - Restart VPS
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/rebuild  - Rebuild/reinstall OS
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/setup    - Initial setup
- GET  /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/metrics   - Resource usage metrics
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/snapshot  - Create snapshot
- GET  /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/snapshots - List snapshots
- DELETE /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/snapshot/{snapId} - Delete snapshot
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/restore-snapshot   - Restore snapshot
- GET  /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/backups   - List backups
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/restore-backup     - Restore backup
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/firewall  - Configure firewall rules
- GET  /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/firewall  - Get firewall rules
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/ssh-key   - Manage SSH keys
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/hostname  - Set hostname
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/change-password  - Change root password
- GET  /api/vps/v1/data-centers               - List available data centers
- GET  /api/vps/v1/os-templates               - List available OS templates
- POST /api/vps/v1/virtual-machines/{id}/ptr-record - Set PTR record

NOTE: Verify exact endpoints at https://developers.hostinger.com — the API has been
evolving and endpoints may have been added/modified in 2026.


2) HOSTINGER VPS FEATURES BEYOND BASIC VPS\[REDACTED_TOKEN]
Features included with Hostinger VPS KVM2 plans:

SNAPSHOTS:
- Manual snapshots via panel or API
- Typically 1 free snapshot slot (can create/delete)
- Instant restore capability

BACKUPS:
- Weekly automated backups (included in most plans)
- Backup restore via panel or API
- Daily backups available on higher plans

FIREWALL:
- Built-in firewall management via hPanel
- Configure rules (allow/deny by port, protocol, IP)
- Manageable via API

DNS MANAGEMENT:
- Full DNS zone management through hPanel for domains hosted there
- A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, SRV records
- PTR/rDNS record setting for VPS IP

DDoS PROTECTION:
- Basic DDoS protection included at infrastructure level
- Cloudflare-based network filtering on some data centers
- Not enterprise-grade; recommended to use Cloudflare in front

MONITORING:
- Basic resource monitoring in hPanel (CPU, RAM, disk, bandwidth)
- Resource usage graphs (historical)
- API-accessible metrics

OS TEMPLATES:
- Multiple Linux distros (Ubuntu, Debian, CentOS, AlmaLinux, Rocky, Fedora)
- Application templates (Docker, LAMP, WordPress, etc.)
- Custom ISO support on some plans

NETWORKING:
- Dedicated IPv4 address (included)
- IPv6 support (varies by data center)
- 1 Gbps network port (KVM2), some plans up to 300 Mbps
- Generous bandwidth (varies by plan, many include TB-level)

SSH KEY MANAGEMENT:
- Upload and manage SSH keys via panel/API

AI ASSISTANT:
- Hostinger integrated an AI assistant in hPanel for troubleshooting
- Kodee AI chatbot for support


3) RECENT HOSTINGER FEATURES & INTEGRATIONS ([REDACTED_PHONE]+)\[REDACTED_TOKEN]
- Public API Launch: Hostinger launched their developer API (developers.hostinger.com)
  enabling programmatic VPS management — this was a major addition
- AI Integration (Kodee): AI-powered assistant integrated across hPanel
- Improved hPanel VPS Section: Redesigned VPS management dashboard
- KVM2 Platform: Upgraded virtualization infrastructure for better performance
- Docker OS Template: Pre-configured Docker template for quick deployment
- Expanded Data Centers: Additional locations (Europe, Asia, North/South America)
- GitHub Integration: Some hosting products got GitHub deployment integration
- Improved Firewall UI: Enhanced firewall rule management in panel
- Hostinger VPS pricing became more competitive (plans starting ~$5-6/month)

KVM2 Plan Tiers (approximate, verify current pricing):
- KVM 1: 1 vCPU, 4 GB RAM, 50 GB NVMe, 4 TB bandwidth
- KVM 2: 2 vCPU, 8 GB RAM, 100 GB NVMe, 8 TB bandwidth
- KVM 4: 4 vCPU, 16 GB RAM, 200 GB NVMe, 16 TB bandwidth
- KVM 8: 8 vCPU, 32 GB RAM, 400 GB NVMe, 32 TB bandwidth


4) BEST PRACTICES: DOCKER SWARM ON HOSTINGER VPS\[REDACTED_TOKEN]
For your setup (Traefik, Portainer, multiple apps, DBs, monitoring):

RESOURCE MANAGEMENT:
- Set memory limits on ALL containers (deploy.resources.limits in compose)
- Reserve memory for critical services (PostgreSQL, Traefik)
- Keep ~10-15% RAM free for OS operations
- Use docker system prune regularly to reclaim disk space

STORAGE:
- Use named volumes (not bind mounts) for database data
- Monitor disk usage — NVMe is fast but limited
- Set log rotation: max-size and max-file in daemon.json
- Consider external backup for critical volumes

NETWORKING:
- Use overlay networks for Swarm service communication
- Isolate networks: separate frontend/backend/monitoring networks
- Traefik on an overlay network with all services that need external access

SECURITY:
- Use Hostinger firewall to restrict ports (only 80, 443, 22 open externally)
- Use Docker secrets for sensitive data (not environment variables)
- Keep Docker and OS updated
- Use non-root users in containers where possible
- Enable automatic security updates (unattended-upgrades)

MONITORING:
- Your monitoring stack should track: CPU, RAM, disk, network, container health
- Set up alerts for disk >85%, RAM >90%
- Monitor Docker Swarm node health

BACKUPS:
- Automate volume backups (cron + tar or restic/borg)
- Don't rely solely on Hostinger snapshots for data backup
- Test restore procedures periodically
- Back up Docker Swarm configs and secrets

PERFORMANCE:
- Use Swarm placement constraints if multi-node
- Set appropriate replica counts
- Use health checks on all services
- Configure Traefik rate limiting for public services

SPECIFIC TO YOUR STACK:
- PostgreSQL: Set shared_buffers, work_mem appropriately for available RAM
- Ollama: This is very resource-heavy; consider running only when needed
  or setting strict memory limits
- Qdrant: Configure WAL and optimizer settings for your data volume
- Evolution API: Keep WhatsApp session data backed up
- Vaultwarden: Critical data — ensure encrypted backups


5) HOSTINGER API DOCUMENTATION
-------------------------------
Primary URL: https://developers.hostinger.com
Alternative: https://developers.hostinger.com/api/vps

To get started:
1. Log into hPanel (hpanel.hostinger.com)
2. Go to Account > API section
3. Generate an API token
4. Use Bearer token auth in requests
5. Rate limits apply (check docs for current limits)

The API supports:
- VPS lifecycle management (start/stop/restart/rebuild)
- Snapshot management (create/restore/delete)
- Backup management (list/restore)
- Firewall configuration
- SSH key management
- Resource monitoring/metrics
- OS template listing
- Data center listing
- PTR record management
- Hostname configuration

Response format: JSON
Auth: Bearer token in Authorization header
Example: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN" https://api.hostinger.com/api/vps/v1/virtual-machines


RECOMMENDATIONS FOR YOUR SETUP (VPS ID: 1396188)
==================================================
1. Enable Hostinger firewall via API/panel - only open ports 80, 443, 22
2. Set up automated snapshots before major changes
3. Use the API for automated health checks and restart capability
4. Ollama is likely your biggest resource consumer - monitor it closely
5. Verify your current plan tier has enough resources for all these services
6. Set up Cloudflare in front for DDoS protection and caching
7. Automate docker volume backups to external storage (S3/B2)
8. Use the Hostinger API metrics endpoint for external monitoring integration

---

ISSUES ENCOUNTERED:
- Both mcp_web_search and mcp_web_extract tools failed with 401 Unauthorized
  errors (Tavily API auth issue). All information above is based on training
  knowledge up to early 2025. Some details may have changed in 2026.
- RECOMMENDATION: Verify current API endpoints at https://developers.hostinger.com
  as the API was relatively new and evolving rapidly.
